verify_against: .devflow/config.schema.json / scripts/update-branch-checkpoint.sh — confirm the key exists and gates the helper.
verify_hint: Confirm update_branch_checkpoints under devflow_implement exists in the config schema and is honored by scripts/update-branch-checkpoint.sh.

claim: loop-exit.md/fixing.md invoke scripts/update-branch-checkpoint.sh and branch on the tokens it prints: UPDATED / UP_TO_DATE / DISABLED / UNVERIFIED / PUSH_REJECTED / CONFLICT / MERGE_IN_PROGRESS.
verify_against: scripts/update-branch-checkpoint.sh — confirm the helper emits exactly these token words and the 'UPDATED <behind>' single-line shape.
verify_hint: Read scripts/update-branch-checkpoint.sh and confirm the set of printed outcome tokens matches the seven the references branch on, and that UPDATED is printed with a trailing behind-count.

claim: fixing.md item 6a invokes scripts/stale-prose-lint.py reading the diff on stdin with --rev HEAD, and routes exit codes 0/1/2, treating any other non-zero as a producer/helper failure.
verify_against: scripts/stale-prose-lint.py — confirm it accepts --rev, reads the diff on stdin, and uses the 0/1/2 exit-code arms described.
verify_hint: Read scripts/stale-prose-lint.py; confirm the --rev argument, stdin diff input, and the exit-code semantics (0 clean, 1 STALE present, 2 internal error).

claim: loop-exit.md invokes lib/efficiency-trace.sh with --mode trace, --persist, and --self-check (plus --workpad-dir/--slug), directly (no bash prefix), and relies on it keeping its committed executable bit.
verify_against: lib/efficiency-trace.sh — confirm it accepts --mode trace, --persist, --self-check, --workpad-dir, --slug and is committed with the executable bit.
verify_hint: Read lib/efficiency-trace.sh (and check its file mode) to confirm all three modes/flags are supported and it is executable.

claim: loop-control.md claims lib/efficiency-trace.jq derives and surfaces loop_role per iteration (iter1->fix; iterN->promoted when N-1's shadow recorded promoted_to_iter_next), and loop-exit.md claims it assigns each subagent a verdict of unique-effective/corroborating/noise/null.
verify_against: lib/efficiency-trace.jq — confirm the loop_role derivation and the four-way subagent verdict taxonomy.
verify_hint: Read lib/efficiency-trace.jq; confirm it derives loop_role from prior-iter shadow promoted_to_iter_next and emits the unique-effective/corroborating/noise/null verdicts.

claim: loop-control.md claims the navigation-stamp operands current_step, current_substep, pending_dispatch are EXCLUDED from ITER_EXPECTED_FIELDS (they are not effectiveness telemetry).
verify_against: lib/efficiency-trace.sh (the ITER_EXPECTED_FIELDS definition) — confirm the three navigation operands are not listed there.
verify_hint: Grep lib/efficiency-trace.sh for ITER_EXPECTED_FIELDS and confirm current_step/current_substep/pending_dispatch are absent from it.

claim: loop-control.md asserts a universal: 'no configuration combination produces a REJECT the fixer is configured to ignore' — via the REJECT-driver widening (effective fix set = findings >= $FIX_THRESHOLD PLUS every finding at/above verdict_severity_threshold).
verify_against: The Step 2 REJECT-driver widening rule in loop-control.md itself, plus /devflow:review's Phase 4.2 verdict/verdict_severity_threshold semantics.
verify_hint: Construct the falsifying case: pick config combos of verdict_severity_threshold vs fix_severity_threshold (e.g. verdict=suggestion/fix=critical; verdict=critical/fix=suggestion) and check whether any REJECT-driver severity can fall outside the widened effective fix set.

claim: shadow-review.md asserts a universal: 'a three-of-four roster is never full coverage' — a shadow pass dispatching only three always-on reviewers (missing devflow:requesting-code-review) can never set coverage: "full".
verify_against: shadow-review.md's expected-roster computation and the coverage:"full" positive-assertion / 1:1-join rules.
verify_hint: Build the falsifying case: a shadow whose reviewers_dispatched is the three non-final always-on agents. Trace the expected-roster superset join test and confirm it forces coverage: not_verified (outcome 3), never full.

claim: loop-exit.md asserts a universal about ordering: 'any recorded UPDATED is necessarily later than that iteration's suite run' — justified by item 4's suite run always preceding item 6's checkpoint WITHIN the same iteration, so the Loop-exit suite rule needs no 'since last suite run' qualifier.
verify_against: fixing.md — the execution ordering of item 4 (Run tests) vs item 6 (fix commit + Checkpoint-3) within one iteration.
verify_hint: Construct the falsifying case: is there ANY iteration path where the Checkpoint-3 helper (item 6) runs and records UPDATED before item 4's suite run in that same iteration? Trace fixing.md's item ordering (item 4 -> item 6/6a) to confirm the suite always precedes the checkpoint, or find a path that violates it.

claim: loop-exit.md's logs-only post-shadow exemption asserts that an empty/errored git diff output is NOT exempt because 'every path is under .devflow/logs/' is vacuously true over an empty list, and therefore fails closed (sets shadow agreement not verified, does not fall through to the delta-review arm).
verify_against: loop-exit.md's Pre-mapping: Post-shadow edit gate — the logs-only exemption branch and its empty/errored-diff fail-closed handling.
verify_hint: Construct the falsifying case: HEAD_NOW != reviewed_sha with git diff --name-only returning an empty/errored list. Confirm the gate treats it as fail-closed (never sets reviewed_sha=HEAD_NOW, never falls through to the delta-review arm), rather than exempting it as vacuously logs-only.
